description: Per-setup strategy coverage expectations for 96260B split-panel multi-setup. Facing is envelope-driven: the setup reaching an envelope-coincident stock flat owns facing (rear, feat 17 / face 322). Front reaches no envelope stock flat, so it faces nothing. Front milling is over-machining: geometry-derived scope grants the front all reachable work, but nothing arbitrates which setup owns geometry both can reach (shop runs the front as a single facing flip). See eval/open_capability_setup_ownership_arbitration.md and eval/open_capability_homolog_map_split_panel.md.

strategies.name: facing
strategies.reason: the only envelope STOCK flat (feat 17 / face 322, the -Y plate face) is reachable solely from the rear's -Z approach; the front's +Z approach reaches no envelope stock flat, so facing is rear-owned

strategies.name: roughing
strategies.reason: OVER-MACHINING (true positive): shop runs the front as a single facing flip; front milling is work the rear/setup-1 owns. Geometry-derived scope grants the front all reachable geometry, but nothing arbitrates setup ownership yet. Fix by arbitrating ownership, not by moving this into expected_present. See eval/open_capability_setup_ownership_arbitration.md.

strategies.name: finishing_floor
strategies.reason: OVER-MACHINING (true positive): front milling is rear/setup-1 work; setup ownership not yet arbitrated. See eval/open_capability_setup_ownership_arbitration.md.

strategies.reason: OVER-MACHINING (true positive): front milling is rear/setup-1 work; setup ownership not yet arbitrated. See eval/open_capability_setup_ownership_arbitration.md.
